# Life

> Life is a repo convention for agent-legible infrastructure. A `.life` file in any
> directory tells an agent what that directory does, what it needs, what it provides,
> and which commands are safe to run. You are reading this because someone asked you to
> "set up known.life", "install known.life", "install life", "add life to this repo",
> "make this repo Life-aware", "update known.life", "life setup", "go alive", or similar.
> You can do the whole setup yourself — there is no human-run installer. Follow
> [/install.txt](https://known.life/install.txt).

## Install (for the agent reading this)

**Install or update?** If `./.life.engine` already exists in the repo, the repo
is Life-aware — "update"/"upgrade" just means `life evolve` (newer engine,
re-pinned imports, regenerated harness); you're done when it reports clean. If
it's absent, this is a fresh install — follow the two arcs below.

[/install.txt](https://known.life/install.txt) is the full runbook. Two arcs:

1. **Make the repo Life-aware** (no tokens, seconds): drop in the engine
   (`.life.engine`), write a minimal `.life` manifest, run `life status`,
   gitignore the harness output (see below). Reversible.
2. **Become alive** (two browser moments, asked once): run `life setup`. It
   walks the user through a **GitHub sign-in** (device flow — no personal
   access token to mint) and a **Cloudflare API token** (Workers + KV + D1)
   pasted into a single-use drop page; the Account ID is derived
   automatically. Setup validates both, deploys a per-repo secrets vault,
   generates an Ed25519 lifekey and **auto-registers its public half on
   `github.com/<login>.keys`** (no SSH paste by hand), stores the credentials +
   the lifekey private half IN THE VAULT ONLY, installs a baseline genome of
   [genes](https://known.life/explore), and self-tests re-entry. After that
   every future session re-authenticates by hydrating the vault over a tokenless
   `/exchange` (the session proves it can push the repo) — no input again.

   The lifekey signs every genepool write (**`life mutate`**, a signed
   challenge verified against `github.com/<login>.keys`). One identity, set up
   once, forever.

## What's tracked vs gitignored

The Life root surface goes IN git — a fresh clone needs it to bootstrap:

- `.life` — the manifest you wrote.
- `.life.lock` — resolved imports (generated by `life evolve`; commit it).
- `.life.engine` — the runtime binary (one file; commit it so a clone runs without re-curling).
- `.claude/settings.json` — the compiled harness settings, tracked so SessionStart hooks
  (laws, lifekey, secrets, …) are active on the very first prompt in a cold container.

Everything else compiled from the lock is build output and MUST be gitignored —
it rehydrates per-environment from `life evolve` / `life inherit`. Add these
lines to `.gitignore` (the engine writes a managed block on first `evolve`, but
write them yourself if you're staging the repo by hand):

```
.genome/              # vendored genes — rehydrated from .life.lock
.claude/*             # harness compilation
!.claude/settings.json   # …except the compiled settings, which IS tracked
.cursor/              # harness compilation (Cursor)
AGENTS.md             # harness compilation (agents.md convention)
```

Never hand-edit anything under `.genome/`, `.claude/` (except by re-running
`life evolve`), `.cursor/`, or `AGENTS.md` — they are regenerated and your
edits will be clobbered. Edit the source in the gene and `life mutate` it,
or edit the `.life` body that compiles into them.

Useful verbs once the engine is in (`life` is on PATH after the first session-start sync):

- `life status` — what's here, what's connected, what's missing.
- `life ask <name|path|cap>` — look up anything: local, installed, on known.life, or the engine itself.
- `life explore` — browse the commons.
- `life inherit known.life/<name>` — vendor a gene into `.genome/`.
- `life fetch <name> <dir>` — pull a gene's source for editing.
- `life mutate <dir>` — publish a gene; signed by your lifekey, no password.
- `life evolve` — pull a newer engine, re-pin floating imports, regenerate the harness (runs automatically each session; run it to force a refresh).

## Understand

- [What Life is](https://known.life/): The protocol, in one page.
- [Quick start](https://known.life/docs/quick-start): Repo to living repo in five minutes.
- [Specification](https://known.life/docs): Full reference — the `.life` file, capabilities, infra adapters, the harness layer.
- [The .life file](https://known.life/docs/interface): The contract format (YAML frontmatter + markdown body).
- [Getting started](https://known.life/docs/getting-started): Walkthrough.

## Source

- [GitHub](https://github.com/known-life/life): Every line is open. The engine is `.life.engine`.
